title: Energizing Chinese Eggplant Stir-fry
description: This energizing dish is a creamy and fermented treat made with egg plants in a whisked mixture, perfect for a quick lunch or snack.
instructions: Slice the eggplant into thin rounds and sprinkle with cornstarch. Set aside.
Mince garlic and finely chop onion, green onions (scallions).
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, water, sugar, olive oil, chili paste (if using), and fermented black beans until smooth and well combined.
Heat a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add 1 tbsp of olive oil and cook the eggplant slices on each side for about 2 minutes per side, until golden brown. Remove from pan and set aside.
In the same pan, add another 1 tbsp of olive oil if needed. Stir-fry garlic and onion for a minute or two until fragrant.
Add the whisked sauce to the pan, stir well to coat all ingredients evenly, then return the eggplant slices back into the pan.
Stir in green onions (scallions) and let everything cook for another 1-2 minutes. The dish is ready when it's heated through and slightly thickened from cornstarch.
Serve hot as a side or main course with steamed rice, noodles, or any preferred accompaniment.
